title: In unstable markets, does AI become more useful… or less reliable?
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
With recent macro conditions (inflation pressure, rate uncertainty, geopolitical noise), markets feel less “structured” than usual.

Which raises an interesting question for AI in trading:

👉 Does it perform better in stable conditions… or in chaotic ones?

On one hand:

* AI can apply consistent logic regardless of emotion
* it doesn’t overreact to news or volatility

On the other:

* if the market becomes more erratic
* patterns become less reliable
* context becomes harder to formalize

So maybe the real question is:

👉 Is AI better at handling structure… or adapting to uncertainty?

Curious to hear from people building or using AI systems:

How do your models behave when the market becomes less predictable?

comments.author: melbkiwi
comments.body:
I’ve found it’s less about AI handling uncertainty and more about whether the system knows when not to trade.

I’ve had EAs that look solid in clean conditions but just drift or churn when things get messy or participation drops off. It wasn’t really an “AI problem”, more that the system was still trying to operate when the conditions didn’t suit it.

Biggest improvement for me was filtering when to stay out rather than trying to make it adapt to everything.

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
That’s a really good point.

It’s easy to frame it as an “AI problem”, but like you said, it’s often more about **regime awareness** than intelligence.

A system can work perfectly in the conditions it was designed for, but the real challenge is knowing when those conditions are no longer present.

And yeah, trying to make a system adapt to everything usually ends up diluting the edge.

Filtering *when not to trade* is probably the most underrated part — especially in low participation or messy conditions where nothing really has follow-through.

Out of curiosity — how do you define those “stay out” conditions? More volatility-based, structure-based, or something else?
